newsSince: Sentiment-driven activity since June 9 rather than a fresh fundamental disclosure. TXN surged ~7% around June 19-21 on a major Wall Street upgrade naming it a top semiconductor pick with raised price targets, reinforcing the data-center narrative (DC revenue ~+90% YoY, now ~10-11% of revenue, and a newly broken-out data-center segment). The bear caveat is that part of the DC surge reflects temporary pull-forward demand as rivals' foundry bottlenecks ease in 2H26.

sources.label: TXN adds data-center segment, guides above consensus (Fintool)

verdicts.rankRationale: No fresh fundamental disclosure since June 9 - the move is an analyst-upgrade/'top pick' rotation that further mainstreams the hidden-AI-power framing, eroding residual alpha on this crowding-sensitive page. Pull-forward risk is a real caveat. Small -1, hold rank 42.
verdicts.bull: Broad analog/power content benefits from AI data-center buildout; DC revenue ~+90% YoY now a distinct segment.
verdicts.neutral: Real AI exposure but story is now consensus after the upgrade wave; alpha mostly priced in.
verdicts.bear: Crowding intensifies on a +71% YTD mega-cap; DC surge partly pull-forward that reverses as rival foundry capacity frees up in 2H26.

verdicts.rankRationale: Through the AI-node bottleneck lens, analog/power remains second-order to HBM, advanced packaging and AI networking. The new DC segment disclosure is a mild positive but no new node-scarcity evidence since June 9; the upgrade is sentiment. Hold rank 72.
verdicts.bull: Internal 300mm capacity gives lead-time/cost edge; new data-center segment shows direct AI compute pull.
verdicts.neutral: Analog/power is supportive but not a true AI-node bottleneck; less direct than HBM/packaging/networking.
verdicts.bear: Not on the critical AI-node path; pull-forward demand and crowding limit incremental node-bottleneck alpha.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Indirect to the AI-node bottleneck; pull-forward

verdicts.rankRationale: Through the co-packaged-optics lens TXN has limited direct CPO alpha - it supplies power/analog content across infrastructure, not optics. No CPO-relevant news since June 9; the upgrade/DC story is orthogonal to CPO. Hold rank 94 to avoid churn.
verdicts.bull: Power/analog content rides overall optical/DC infrastructure growth, including CPO-era platforms.
verdicts.neutral: Peripheral to CPO; benefits are indirect via broad infrastructure spend.
verdicts.bear: No direct CPO exposure; industrial/auto cycle and post-run-up crowding dominate; limited CPO-specific alpha.
verdicts.primaryRisk: No direct CPO leverage; industrial/auto cycle
